text: The video displays a side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis during a laparoscopic right hemicolectomy, initially viewed under standard white light. Near-infrared laparoscopic fluorescence is then used to confirm the adequacy of perfusion at the anastomosis site.

The surgeon administers 5 milliliters of indocyanine green via a peripheral cannula, resulting in fluorescence uptake at the ileocolic anastomosis and throughout the small bowel, serving as a positive control.
The near-infrared fluorescence imaging confirms satisfactory perfusion of the ileocolic anastomosis, marking the conclusion of the laparoscopic right hemicolectomy.
